content.problemStatement:
JJ has a magical sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. The group of high magicians Sed wants to buy this sequence from him. They calculate its cost as follows:


Choose a subarray of length atleast 222, i.e. a contiguous subsequence Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_rAl‚Äã,Al+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ar‚Äã such that 1‚â§l<r‚â§N1 \le l \lt r \le N1‚â§l<r‚â§N.
The cost is the difference between the largest and the second largest element in this subarray (note that if there is more than one element with the maximum value, the cost is 000).
Clearly, the cost is not uniquely determined, but depends on the choice of the subarray in the first step. JJ is careful, so he wants to find the number of different possible amounts of money he can obtain, i.e. the size of the set of all possible costs for all valid choices of this subarray.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (30 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1035 \cdot 10^35‚ãÖ103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-seperated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of possible costs.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: [1,2][1, 2][1,2] is the only possible subarray that satisfies l<rl \lt rl<r. For this subarray, the cost is 2‚àí1=12 - 1 = 12‚àí1=1. The set of all possible costs is {1}\{1\}{1} and the answer is its size, 111.
Example case 2: There are three subarray that can be chosen: [5,1][5, 1][5,1], [1,3][1, 3][1,3] and [5,1,3][5, 1, 3][5,1,3]. The costs for them are 444, 222 and 222 respectively. Hence, the set of possible costs is {2,4}\{2, 4\}{2,4} and the answer is its size, 222.
